entry_point: DllMain (C:/BOZ-Client-RoF2/src/dllmain_rof2.cpp:6360) handles DLL_PROCESS_ATTACH only: it spins up a worker thread `CreateThread(InitThread)` and returns TRUE immediately (no work done on the loader lock). InitThread (line 6197) is the real bootstrap: (1) calls ResolveBase() (line 570) which does `g_baseAddr = (DWORD)GetModuleHandleA(NULL)` to get eqgame.exe's runtime base; if it fails, logs and aborts. (2) Every absolute address is a constant minus the image base 0x00400000 stored as a relative offset (e.g. `constexpr DWORD FnDspChat = 0x0051F1A0 - 0x00400000;`), then converted at runtime by `Resolve(off) = g_baseAddr + off` (lines 566-568). So the whole module is ASLR-rebase-safe: image-base 0x400000 is baked into the offset constants, runtime base comes from GetModuleHandle(NULL). (3) It then installs hooks: dsp_chat FIRST (pre-patch-loop, line 6212), then a long byte-patch loop for the non-marketplace cross-class/cooldown features (NOT needed by a marketplace-only carve-out), then the per-frame draw hook InstallHook_PwDraw() near line 6291. The marketplace WndNotification hook is NOT installed in InitThread; it is installed lazily on first marketplace-window-open from inside the per-frame draw hook (TickMarketplace -> InstallMktVTableHook). For a STANDALONE marketplace ASI the InitThread body collapses to: ResolveBase(), InstallHook_DspChat(), InstallHook_PwDraw(). The byte-patch loop, SkillRow hook, 870C60 master-UI hook, cascade-menu hook, Node Fight window, and anti-cheat scanner are all droppable.
build_flags: x86 / Win32 ONLY (CMakeLists.txt FATAL_ERRORs if CMAKE_SIZEOF_VOID_P==8; configure with `cmake -A Win32`). C++17 (CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17, REQUIRED ON). Static CRT: CMAKE_MSVC_RUNTIME_LIBRARY = "MultiThreaded$<$<CONFIG:Debug>:Debug>" (i.e. /MT, /MTd) so the .asi has zero external runtime deps (no UCRT / api-ms-win-*). Output: SHARED library with OUTPUT_NAME "BOZ", SUFFIX ".asi", PREFIX "" -> "BOZ.asi" (standalone should pick a distinct name, e.g. "BOZMkt.asi", to avoid colliding with the full BOZ.asi in the same client dir). Compile defs: WIN32_LEAN_AND_MEAN, NOMINMAX, _CRT_SECURE_NO_WARNINGS. Libs: the ONLY linked lib is `psapi` (target_link_libraries ... psapi), and per the comment at CMakeLists.txt:35-37 it is pulled in EXCLUSIVELY for EnumProcessModules / GetModuleBaseNameA used by the anti-cheat module scanner -- the MARKETPLACE code does not use psapi at all. A marketplace-only carve-out that drops the anti-cheat scanner needs NO extra link libs (kernel32 is implicit). Everything else (VirtualAlloc/Protect, GetModuleHandle, CreateThread, HeapAlloc) is kernel32, auto-linked. Loaded by an ASI loader (ASI Loader / dinput8 shim) that LoadLibrary's *.asi from the client dir.

hook_installs.hook: Per-frame draw hook (CPlayerWnd::Draw) — the marketplace's main-thread tick driver; calls TickMarketplace() every UI frame
hook_installs.mechanism: Inline 5-byte E9 JMP detour with a VirtualAlloc'd trampoline. Steals 10 bytes of the function prologue (verified to be 56 8B F1 0F B6 86 80 02 00 00 = PUSH ESI; MOV ESI,ECX; MOVZX EAX,[ESI+0x280]), copies them to a 32-byte RWX trampoline, appends E9 JMP back to target+10, then overwrites target with E9 rel32 -> MyHook_PwDraw + five 0x90 NOP pad. __thiscall captured via __fastcall (ECX=this, dummy EDX). FlushInstructionCache after patching.
hook_installs.target_addr: 0x00718CF0 absolute (Off::FnPlayerWndDraw = 0x00718CF0 - 0x00400000 = 0x318CF0, resolved via Resolve()). NOTE: for a marketplace-only build this hook is still required ONLY because it is the cheapest reliable main-thread per-frame tick; TickMarketplace + the marketplace funds/tile/buy upkeep all run from here. The mana-bar widget poke inside MyHook_PwDraw (offsets +0x22C/+0x238/+0x244) is non-marketplace and can be stripped.
hook_installs.install_site: InstallHook_PwDraw() at C:/BOZ-Client-RoF2/src/dllmain_rof2.cpp:2057 (hook body MyHook_PwDraw at :2022). Called from InitThread at :6291.

hook_installs.hook: dsp_chat chat-display hook (CEverQuest::dsp_chat) — parses [BOZ_MKT_*] catalog feed rows from the server, suppresses them from chat, and feeds the marketplace catalog cache
hook_installs.mechanism: Inline 5-byte E9 JMP detour with VirtualAlloc'd trampoline. Steals 6 prologue bytes (verified prologue MOV EAX,FS:[0] = 64 A1 00 00 00 00), copies to a 32-byte RWX trampoline, appends E9 JMP back to target+6; overwrites target with E9 rel32 -> MyHook_DspChat + one 0x90 NOP. dsp_chat is __thiscall(this, text, color, echo, brackets), RET 0x10. FlushInstructionCache after.
hook_installs.install_site: InstallHook_DspChat() at C:/BOZ-Client-RoF2/src/dllmain_rof2.cpp:3909 (hook body MyHook_DspChat referenced at :3951). Called from InitThread at :6212 (installed FIRST, before any byte patches).

hook_installs.hook: Marketplace WndNotification hook (CMarketplaceWnd::WndNotification, vtable slot 34) — intercepts tile-selects + the Buy/Inspect button clicks for click-to-buy
hook_installs.mechanism: Per-INSTANCE vtable swap (NOT an inline detour, NOT IAT). On first open it reads the window's vtable ptr, HeapAlloc's a 256-slot copy (HEAP_ZERO_MEMORY), memcpy's the original vtable into it (SEH-guarded), saves orig slot 34 into g_origMktWndNotif, writes &MyHook_MktWndNotification into copy[34], then repoints wnd[0] (the object's vtable pointer) at the copy. Slot 34 = CSidlScreenWnd::WndNotification (== vtable +0x88), verified empirically. __thiscall via __fastcall.
hook_installs.target_addr: RUNTIME object address, not a fixed code RVA. The marketplace window object is found each frame via the global CMarketplaceWnd* at absolute 0x00D1FD08 (MktOff::pWnd = 0x00D1FD08 - 0x00400000 = 0x91FD08; `window = *(DWORD*)Resolve(MktOff::pWnd)`), gated on its visible byte at +0x196 == 1. The hooked slot is window-vtable[34]; the original handler pointer is whatever orig vtable[34] held.
hook_installs.install_site: InstallMktVTableHook(DWORD wnd) at C:/BOZ-Client-RoF2/src/dllmain_rof2.cpp:5039 (hook body MyHook_MktWndNotification at :4975). Installed lazily, once per window-open, from TickMarketplace at :3019-3021 (`if (window != g_mktHookedWnd) { InstallMktVTableHook(window); g_mktHookedWnd = window; ... }`), which itself runs from the per-frame MyHook_PwDraw via TickMarketplace() at :2047. So this hook depends on the draw hook being installed first.

notes: Carve-out summary: a standalone marketplace ASI needs only 3 things at bootstrap — (a) the rebase plumbing (g_baseAddr/Resolve/ResolveBase, offset constants expressed as `ABS - 0x00400000`), (b) the dsp_chat trampoline (catalog feed in), and (c) the PwDraw trampoline (per-frame tick that drives TickMarketplace, which in turn installs the marketplace vtable hook on demand). The marketplace WndNotification hook installs itself from the draw tick, so no extra InitThread wiring is required for it. NOTHING in the marketplace path uses psapi — drop the `target_link_libraries(... psapi)` line entirely once the anti-cheat scanner is excluded; that is the only declared lib. Keep build flags identical: x86/Win32, C++17, /MT static CRT, .asi suffix, but change OUTPUT_NAME so the standalone doesn't overwrite the full BOZ.asi. Two of the three hooks (PwDraw, dsp_chat) are inline E9-JMP trampoline detours over an FS:[0]-style/standard prologue with prologue-byte verification before patching; the third (marketplace) is a per-instance vtable[34] swap on a runtime-discovered object, not a code patch. There are byte-patch/PatchOneByte/PatchBytes JMP writers in the file (PatchDispatcherJG etc.) but ALL of them belong to the non-marketplace cross-class/cooldown feature set and are not part of the marketplace bootstrap. Both trampoline installers VirtualAlloc a 32-byte RWX trampoline, VirtualProtect the target to RWX for the patch, restore protection, and FlushInstructionCache. ASLR safety comes entirely from GetModuleHandle(NULL)+offset rebasing; image base 0x00400000 is only ever a compile-time subtrahend.
